title: tragopogon gorskianus
intro: Tragopogon gorskianus, also known as the Gorskian salsify or goat's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's root and leaves have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Tragopogon gorskianus can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its flowers and seeds can be used as a food source, particularly in traditional Balkan cuisine.
